title: DIGITAL DESIGN & RTL
copy: Currently focused on RTL design patterns in Verilog, timing analysis, and synthesis constraints. Building fluency in writing synthesizable code vs simulation-only constructs.

title: FPGA DEVELOPMENT
copy: Hands-on with Xilinx Basys3 and Altera DE10-Lite. Learning Vivado and Quartus flows. Understanding the gap between behavioral simulation and actual hardware behavior.

title: EMBEDDED SYSTEMS
copy: Exploring bare-metal programming on ARM Cortex-M. Learning to read datasheets, configure peripherals via registers, and write drivers without HAL abstractions.

title: SIGNAL PROCESSING
copy: Building foundations in DSP - sampling theory, filter design, frequency domain analysis. Applying concepts in MATLAB before moving to hardware implementation.

title: COMPUTER ARCHITECTURE
copy: Starting to understand processor pipelines, memory hierarchies, and the hardware-software boundary. Reading Patterson & Hennessy alongside coursework.
